caption: Copper got into the trash again last night. Third time this month. Starting to think he's smarter than both of us.
Dad he literally has one brain cell lol. Did you at least get a picture?
Didn't think to. Was 0530 and I hadn't had coffee yet. He looked pleased with himself.
ok but that's kind of funny. How's the shop? Bench backed up again?
Commission list is heavier than usual this week. Nothing I can't manage. Eddie's been solid on the stone supply.
Good. Are you eating real food or just gas station coffee and whatever's dying in the break room fridge?
Made eggs this morning. That counts.
Barely counts and you know it. Come visit soon, I'll actually cook something. Miss you.
Finished the Ruiz cuff. Heavy gauge sterling, single Sleeping Beauty turquoise. Dad would've liked this one.
Kai took the shot at the parade. Copper got more attention than the float. Fair enough.
Morning walk out past the Red Rock Range. Coffee's better when you drink it outside.
Gallup skyline at dusk off Route 66. Martha sent this one over. Put it on the shop wall.
New turquoise slab from Eddie. Cleanest matrix I've cut in months. Headed into a bolo tie.
Copper in the hat again. Rita says it's a morale boost. I say it's a dog in a hat. Both can be true.
Kai had daylight on the whole pack at the meet. Good kid, good legs. Frame this one.
Belt buckle order for the Sedona gallery is boxed and ready to ship. Spiny oyster on this one came out right.
Royston cab set into a heavy cuff this week. Matrix runs warm against the sterling. Listing it on the shop tonight.
Stamped a row of feathers along the band by hand. Pop's old stamps still cut clean after all these years.
Lapis pendant finished and polished. Quiet blue stone, plain bezel. Sometimes less work on the metal is the right call.
Booth set for the Gallup craft fair this weekend. Cuffs, bolos, a few buckles. Come by if you are in town.
Cut a fresh batch of cabochons off the saw. Kingman on the left, Royston on the right. Long night at the bench ahead.
